institutional_exposure.label: Intimate Surveillance
institutional_exposure.summary: learned that the shortest path to control is to make observation feel like devotion
institutional_exposure.financial: side-channel exposure to device access, shared-account leverage, and dependency economics
institutional_exposure.operational: long exposure to location awareness, secret archiving, and memory dispute management

institutional_exposure.predatory: repeatedly exposed others to closeness as a monitoring technology

core_competencies.summary: can move fault across rooms, roles, and reporting lines until nobody feels authorized to hold it

macguffin.public_myth: a fragment of first authority capable of overruling reality itself
macguffin.real_function: lets one person impose a single answer on complex worlds that should remain contested

macguffin.misread_by: people who think infinite stakes are the same thing as meaningful stakes
macguffin.activation_condition: requires a synchronized chamber, a willing conduit, and enough ego to call it mercy
macguffin.use_cost: each use makes every smaller human relationship feel disposable
macguffin.containment_needs: radiation shielding, theological language, and very expensive silence
macguffin.betrayal_magnet: the believer who learns they were always just the battery

macguffin.portability: transportable only inside a panic-worthy escort chain
macguffin.custody_state: split between vault custody and whispered founder mythology
macguffin.side_effects: causes immediate sky-beam behavior in weak scripts

workshop_pitch: You selected Seductive Deceiver plus Intimacy Parasite and got a villain whose favorite workplace protection is seduction. Their primary moral texture is shared space disrespect. Their exposure lateral is Intimate Surveillance. Their core competency stack is Blame Laundering. They are currently fixated on The Veto Shard. Their sidekick is a publicist of the end times who stays because they are too tired to resist. Their formal training comes from The School of Therapeutic Power Abuse.
opening_image: A room full of reflections suggests intimacy was staged here long before you arrived.
dossier_summary: This build runs on misdirection inside a private suite. At the story level, it turns intimacy into the delivery system for danger. The primary moral texture is shared space disrespect. The internal lie is simple: honesty is just timing. The exposure profile is intimate surveillance, which operationalized into blame laundering. The current prize object is The Veto Shard, publicly sold as a fragment of first authority capable of overruling reality itself and privately used to lets one person impose a single answer on complex worlds that should remain contested. The local ecosystem includes a publicist of the end times who handles social media. Before any grand plan begins, the daily proof of rot is not replacing the toilet paper, but the full civilian rot profile also includes microwaving fish at work, placing the new toilet paper on top of the empty roll instead of replacing it, putting the empty carton back in the fridge.
public_facing_bio: Publicly, this figure presents as polished, necessary, and strangely inevitable. Privately, they are a pure engine of shared space disrespect shaped by intimate surveillance and confident enough to treat blame laundering like a normal leadership behavior. Their chosen prize, The Veto Shard, lets them pretend the whole thing is about destiny instead of appetite. The publicist of the end times nearby makes the whole thing feel alarmingly maintainable.
internal_memo: Internal note: continue protecting the principal from low-level reputation damage tied to not replacing the toilet paper. Pattern review now includes microwaving fish at work, placing the new toilet paper on top of the empty roll instead of replacing it, putting the empty carton back in the fridge. Their current strengths remain blame laundering, strategic ambiguity, documentation weaponization. MacGuffin containment around The Veto Shard remains active; public language should stay with a fragment of first authority capable of overruling reality itself, not lets one person impose a single answer on complex worlds that should remain contested. The publicist of the end times remains responsible for social media and is currently too tired to resist. Do not mention the Committee for Licensed Bureaucratic Cruelty matter in writing.
signature_scene: The villain stages a scene inside the private suite where seduction briefly passes for order until the room notices too late that the real agenda is this: colonizes trust before making a move. At the center of the scene sits The Veto Shard, which claims to be a fragment of first authority capable of overruling reality itself but really exists to lets one person impose a single answer on complex worlds that should remain contested. The publicist of the end times quietly handles social media in the background, which is why the whole thing almost works. Somewhere in the room, somebody realizes too late that the sidekick is surprisingly capable and waiting for a better offer.
fake_trailer: In a world shaped by corporate satire, one seductive deceiver build will learn that intimacy must be recognized as infrastructure and exited deliberately. Exposure: Intimate Surveillance. Core competencies: blame laundering, strategic ambiguity. Prize object: The Veto Shard. Sidekick: publicist of the end times on social media. Somebody is definitely not replacing the toilet paper in act one, but the real warning is that the rot profile also includes microwaving fish at work, placing the new toilet paper on top of the empty roll instead of replacing it, putting the empty carton back in the fridge.
sequel_slop: Franchise extension: somebody rebuilds the same logic with fresher branding, and the receipts get harder to kill. The petty tell survives too, because evil this scalable still finds time for not replacing the toilet paper. The wider rot profile survives too: microwaving fish at work, placing the new toilet paper on top of the empty roll instead of replacing it, putting the empty carton back in the fridge. So does The Veto Shard, because objects like that never stay buried once institutions learn the cover story. Another publicist of the end times arrives, still too tired to resist, still handling social media.
sample_lines: If not replacing the toilet paper bothers you, wait until you hear the rest of my rot profile. Then meet my publicist of the end times
My core competencies include blame laundering, and my publicist of the end times handles social media.
If this sounds compassionate, you are already inside the problem.
hooks: The villain is less interested in victory than in teaching the room a worse definition of normal.

Even before the grand scheme, the villain was already the kind of person who would be not replacing the toilet paper, and the wider rot profile includes microwaving fish at work, placing the new toilet paper on top of the empty roll instead of replacing it, putting the empty carton back in the fridge.
The audience trusts the build less the moment they learn even the publicist of the end times is hanging around because they are too tired to resist.
